title: Conjecture 1 (Hadwiger's Conjecture)
statement_text: Let $t \in \mathbb{N}$. If a graph $G$ is $K_t$-minor-free, then $\chi(G) \leq t-1$.
context_text: Hadwiger's coloring conjecture, first stated in 1943, is among the most famous open problems in graph theory. It claims a deep relationship between chromatic number and graph minor containment. The paper studies the list coloring analog, showing that a corresponding statement for the list chromatic number fails for large classes of graphs $H$.
paper_contribution: The paper shows that a list coloring extension of Hadwiger's conjecture fails broadly: for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and all sufficiently large graphs $H$, $f_\ell(H) \geq (1-\varepsilon)(v(H)+\kappa(H))$, which exceeds the conjectured chromatic bound $v(H)-1$ by a constant factor for all large graphs of linear connectivity.

title: Conjecture 2 (H-Hadwiger Conjecture)
context_text: The $H$-Hadwiger conjecture, proposed by Seymour, generalizes Hadwiger's conjecture by replacing $K_t$ with an arbitrary graph $H$ on $t$ vertices. The paper's central aim is to explore the limits of a list coloring extension of this conjecture by proving new lower bounds on $f_\ell(H)$.
paper_contribution: Proves that $f_\ell(H) \geq (1-\varepsilon)(v(H)+\kappa(H))$ for all sufficiently large graphs $H$, and that asymptotically almost every $n$-vertex graph $H$ with $\lceil Cn\log n\rceil$ edges satisfies $f_\ell(H) \geq (2-\varepsilon)n$, showing that a list coloring analog of the $H$-Hadwiger conjecture fails widely.

title: Problem 1
statement_text: Characterize the class $\mathcal{H}$ of graphs $H$ such that $f_\ell(H') = v(H')-1$ for all $H' \subseteq H$.
context_text: The authors ask for a structural characterization of graphs $H$ for which the list chromatic number of $H$-minor-free graphs matches the trivial lower bound $v(H)-1$. The problem is posed in the spirit of perfect graph theory, seeking the largest subgraph-closed class with this property; the paper's main results (Theorems 2 and 4) strongly limit the horizon for positive instances.
